query: slots, pins, and grooves vs other retention methods in abutment teeth

retrieved_docs.title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: GICs are highly successful as luting agents for cementation of stainless steel crowns for deciduous teeth, cast metal crowns, fixed dental prostheses for permanent teeth, space maintainers, orthodontic bands, and brackets. Resin-modified GIC is used for luting zirconia and alumina-based crowns. GICs can repair defects in tooth structure before crown preparation to stabilize the weakened portions of the tooth.

retrieved_docs.title: Overview of Periodontal Surgical Procedures -- Indications -- Clinical Crown Lengthening
retrieved_docs.content: Bone removal can expose sufficient sound tooth structure to allow restoration: Bone removal allows the preparation of axial walls that are at least 4 mm long Bone removal permits enough occlusal reduction for minimal restorative material thickness For root-canal treated teeth, bone removal allows the preparation of 1 mm ferrule Bone removal creates sufficient space for soft tissue attachment and a minimal sulcus (average 3 mm) Long enough bone-supported root structure to permit a few millimeters of bone removal without resulting in a poor crown: root ratio or tooth mobility Bone removal does not expose nearby furcation entrances. Longer tooth appearance and gingival recession do not pose a significant aesthetic concern.

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Mini-Implants -- Technique or Treatment
retrieved_docs.content: Preoperative planning includes a maximum of diagnostic information. A panoramic x-ray is a minimum requirement, a Cone Beam CT scan is recommended for 3D planning especially in cases with very narrow ridges. Raising a flap or flapless; If there is sufficient width of the ridge a flapless transgingival technique for the pilot drill is possible. When however a narrow ridge of extensive soft tissue is present a minimal flap (crestal incision) is recommended to reveal the bone. This would allow the exact placement of the implants at the correct angulation in the bone. The mini dental implant system utilizes a self-tapping threaded screw design and employs minimally invasive surgical intervention. For successful osteointegration and high success rate, initial stability of the mini implant is important and is dependent upon the bone quality, implant design, and surgical technique used. [1] Failure of the mini implant-supported maxillary removable prosthesis is attributed to the facial angulation of maxillary implants, thick masticatory mucosa requiring longer implant abutments, and disparallelism of the unsplinted implants producing micromovements. [14] Retention rings of the mini implant-supported overdenture should be periodically changed. [1] A torque of 35 N/cm is used during implantation of the mini dental implants with a ratchet. [1] It is important to reline the mini implant-supported removable complete prosthesis periodically and to perform occlusal adjustments for appropriate force and movement distribution to avoid implant fracture and overload-induced bone loss around the implants. [15] [16] [17] Implant placement involves the following procedure: The left and right mental foramen are marked with an intra-oral skin marker. The ridge is marked 7 mm anterior of the mental foramen to indicate the most distal implant size. This safety zone includes a potentially present 3 to 5 mm anterior loop and a 2 mm security margin.

retrieved_docs.title: Stainless Steel Crowns in Primary Dentition -- Technique or Treatment -- Crown Adaptation
retrieved_docs.content: Place the crown linguo-buccally on the prepared tooth, applying pressure in the buccal direction so the crown slips over the buccal surface and into the gingival sulcus. There is friction as the crown slides over the buccal bulge. Crown positioning be difficult if the crown is too small or if the buccal bulge is too excessive. Choose a larger crown or reduce the tooth's buccal bulge in this case. After placing the selected crown over the prepared tooth, ask the patient to bite over it. Check for preliminary occlusal and marginal ridge relation. Previously, scratch a mark on the stainless steel crown at the level of the free gingival margin. Scratch or dotted lines indicate the gingival contour and the portion of the crown to be removed. Remove the crown and trim or cut away the additional crown portion gingivally 1 mm below the scratch line with a crown and bridge scissors or, preferably, a large abrasive wheel (“heatless stone”) that allows for more precise reduction. Repeat the procedure until the adequate extension of 1 mm beneath the gingival margin is achieved.

retrieved_docs.title: Stainless Steel Crowns in Primary Dentition -- Technique or Treatment -- Cementation
retrieved_docs.content: A luting cement should be used to secure the crown. Once the crown is seated on the prepared tooth, it should be kept under pressure while the cement hardens. Manufacturers recommend glass ionomer, zinc polycarboxylate, and zinc phosphate cement but fluoride-leaching cement has additional advantages. Due to the clinical advantages, RMGIC is the preferred choice. [22] However, research suggests the type of cement used has little effect on retention, with the essential retentive components derived from proper crown contouring and crimping. Operators must pay deliberate attention to the removal of excess cement. This is normally accomplished by running a pointed instrument around the cemented crown's margins and sliding knotted dental floss buccolingually through the contact areas after the cement setting. Excess cement has been shown to affect gingival health. [8] [6]

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Implants -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: Organized dentistry has finally accepted implant dentistry. The current trend to expand the use of implant dentistry will continue until every restorative practice uses this modality for abutment support of both fixed and removable prostheses on a regular basis as the primary option for all tooth replacements.

retrieved_docs.title: Zirconia Biomaterials -- Issues of Concern
retrieved_docs.content: In dentistry, with the development of computer-aided design (CAD)/computeraided manufacturing (CAM) systems, high strength zirconia can be used for the fabrication of crowns, veneers, fixed partial dentures, posts and cores, implants, and implant abutments. In addition, different auxiliary components such as cutting burs and surgical drills, extra-coronal attachments, and orthodontic brackets manufactured using zirconia are available commercially. [7] [8]

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Mini-Implants -- Anatomy and Physiology
retrieved_docs.content: Mini dental implants can be compared to conventional implant systems. They are made of one piece; however, conventional implants usually consist of two parts, the implant, and the abutment. Mini implants have a one-piece titanium screw with a ball-shaped head for denture stabilization or a square prosthetic head for fixed applications, instead of the classic abutment. A bracket like head design can aid in orthodontic treatment and serve as indirect anchorage. Mini implants are protruded over the gum surface when they are placed into the bone; conventional implants are placed under the gums. Transmucosal portion of the neck of mini-implant should be smooth and varies in length depending upon the mucosal thickness of the implant site. [7] [8] [9] [10]

retrieved_docs.title: Gadolinium Magnetic Resonance Imaging -- Contraindications
retrieved_docs.content: It is important to note that some items, such as certain neurostimulation systems, medication pumps, and cardiac pacemakers, are acceptable for MRI. However, the radiologist and MRI technologist must know the appropriate type that patients have in order to ensure safety. Patients and other individuals must remove the following items before entering the MRI scanner room: Any article of clothing that has metallic fibers or threads, metallic zippers, buttons, snaps, hooks, or underwire Hair barrettes, hairpins Beepers and cell phones Hearing aids Paper clips, coins, pens, keys Belt buckles, shoes, safety pins Watches, metallic jewelry Purse, money clip, wallet, cards with magnetic strips, credit/debit cards The following objects, if near the areas being imaged, may interfere with the image quality generated: Dental fillings (may distort images of the brain or facial areas; the same is true for orthodontic retainers and braces)

retrieved_docs.title: Stainless Steel Crowns in Primary Dentition -- Technique or Treatment -- Procedure
retrieved_docs.content: Place orthodontic separators between the contact point of the primary molar for 4 or 5 days to create room for the crown. Select the smallest-sized crown covering all the cusps, approaching the contact points, and giving a 'spring back' effect. Adjustments of the crown are to be made using band-forming pliers if necessary. Cementation of the crown with glass ionomer cement. The tooth must be maintained in occlusion until the cement sets, followed by excess removal using floss. [25] [26] The Hall technique should be chosen as an alternative when the traditional approach with crown preparation is implausible. [25]

retrieved_docs.title: Overview of Periodontal Surgical Procedures -- Complications -- Crown Lengthening Surgeries
retrieved_docs.content: Teeth that have undergone crown lengthening surgeries will appear longer than before, and the interdental papilla will no longer reach the original interproximal contact. Therefore, restorations must account for these changes by having longer interproximal contacts. Teeth undergoing crown lengthening surgery must also be provisionalized for at least 6 weeks after crown lengthening. Patients must be instructed on how to perform oral hygiene for provisional restorations and prevent accidental fracture or dislodgment of provisional restorations. Since crown lengthening removes crestal bone and available bone height to vital structures, in rare cases, crown lengthening may preclude future implant therapy as there may no longer be enough bone for the placement of implants. In the anterior maxilla, crown lengthening of teeth on one side may require matching crown lengthening on the other side to achieve an esthetic result.

retrieved_docs.title: Posterior Crossbite -- Treatment / Management -- Management of Posterior Crossbite
retrieved_docs.content: NiTi Expanders: These nickel-titanium wire shapes can be attached to a lingual sheath welded to molar bands cemented to the maxillary first permanent molars. Various sizes are available and must be selected depending on the amount of expansion desired and the pretreatment width of the palate.

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Implants -- Complications
retrieved_docs.content: The most common postoperative complication is incision line opening. The design of the removable interim prosthesis is involved, it is corrected. The patient is instructed to rinse 2-3 times daily with chlorhexidine. If granulation process extends for more than two weeks, epithelial margin trimming can be done. If implants become exposed during the healing period, no attempt should be made to cover them with tissue. Rather denture is relieved aggressively over the area with implant exposure. The mobility of the implant during healing is unusual but may occur, mostly accompanied by a radiolucent zone around the implant. Whatever may be the cause, the implant should be removed. Signs and symptoms of failure for an implant are horizontal mobility  greater than 0.5 mm, rapid progressive bone loss, pain during percussion, uncontrolled exudate, generalized radiolucency around the implant, more than one half of the bone is lost around the implant and last the implants inserted in poor position, making them useless for prosthetic support. A success rate of 85%at the end of 5 year period and 80% at the end of 10 year period are minimum criteria for success. [15] [16] [17] [18]

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Implants -- Equipment
retrieved_docs.content: The armamentarium (comprising equipment and different parts of an implant) used for the surgical placement of a dental implant is as follows: Disposable syringes Disposable surgical blades Towel clips Periosteal elevator Dental implant surgical kit (containing different drills) Physiodispenser with the surgical handpiece Dental implant Cover screw Healing abutment Needle holder Toothed tissue holding forceps Scissors, surgical sutures (in flap surgery) Soft tissue punch (for flapless surgeries) Types of implants and implant materials Endosteal Subperiosteal Transosteal

retrieved_docs.title: Stainless Steel Crowns in Primary Dentition -- Equipment -- Others
retrieved_docs.content: Rough or whitening polish wheels Greenstone or heatless stone/rubber wheel stone for finishing and polishing [11] Wire wheel for finishing crown Cement medium - glass slab/cement mixing pad, spatula, cement Rubber dam armamentarium Local anesthesia Dental floss Pliers and instruments Sharp explorer for marking the gingival extension of the crown margin

retrieved_docs.title: Oral Surgery, Extraction of Roots -- Indications
retrieved_docs.content: Retained dental roots may present with one or many indications for removal. Some of the most common indications for the extraction of root fragments are: The presence of acute or chronic infection Partial or complete fracture of the crown Future dental implant placement Pain Vertical root fractures Periodontal disease Caries Presence of pathology Future prosthetic restoration of the site with a fixed or removable prosthesis Preservation of vital adjacent teeth and structures Patient esthetic concerns

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 2 -- Community 1198
retrieved_docs.content: Nasogastric tube procedures classify the nasogastric tube, indicating that the range of clinical actions, techniques, and protocols are organized around the use, insertion, management, and care of the nasogastric tube.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 16 -- Level 3 -- Community 1893
retrieved_docs.content: Implantation procedure is classified under the broader category of التنبيب (intubation). The Insertion of catheter into esophagus, permanent tube type (procedure) represents a more specific, narrower form of التنبيب, indicating a specialized application of intubation techniques. Transglottic catheterization of trachea employs التنبيب as its procedural method, illustrating that this airway access technique is performed through intubation. Together, these relationships highlight how various catheter‑based interventions are organized under the overarching concept of التنبيب, with some procedures serving as parent categories, others as specialized subtypes, and certain techniques directly utilizing intubation as their operative method.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2909
retrieved_docs.content: Delivery by double application of forceps is a obstetric procedure in which the fetal head serves as the direct site of the intervention.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 3 -- Community 2325
retrieved_docs.content: The trachea serves as the anatomical site for tracheostomy procedures, and these procedures may be performed as either temporary or permanent interventions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2341
retrieved_docs.content: The mentum presentation of the fetus, in which the fetal chin is the leading part during delivery, is classified as a مّجيء وَجهي (face presentation). Recognizing this presentation is important for obstetric assessment and delivery planning, as it influences the choice of birth method and the management of potential complications.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2813
retrieved_docs.content: Ultrasonography of head and neck is classified as a form of diagnostic ultrasound of the head and neck.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 9 -- Level 3 -- Community 1691
retrieved_docs.content: Respiratory intubation and mechanical ventilation is recognized as both the specific medical procedure and the category that defines it, effectively classifying itself within the domain of airway management and ventilatory support.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 17 -- Level 3 -- Community 2148
retrieved_docs.content: Respiratory intubation and mechanical ventilation is classified as تركيب مسلك هوائي فموي بلعومي, indicating that this procedure involves the insertion of an oral and pharyngeal airway to establish and maintain a secure airway for assisted ventilation.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 2 -- Community 752
retrieved_docs.content: essential for clinicians to anticipate potential delivery complications, decide when normal delivery procedures are feasible, and implement appropriate interventions to optimize maternal and neonatal outcomes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2791
retrieved_docs.content: Episiotomy and تمزق الأغشية الاصطناعي (artificial membrane rupture) are both classified under الإجراءات العلاجية الخاصة بالتوليد (obstetric therapeutic procedures). By recognizing these interventions as subcomponents of the broader category of obstetric therapeutic care, clinicians can better organize treatment options, understand their related indications, and apply appropriate management strategies within the field of obstetrics.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 1 -- Community 209
retrieved_docs.content: constitute major complications of pregnancy, childbirth, and the puerperium. Recognizing the placement of each specific condition within its broader category supports comprehensive assessment, risk stratification, and coordinated management of pregnant patients.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2421
retrieved_docs.content: Antepartum Procedures is a medical category that includes, as its child, the subcategory Antepartum Procedures: Application, Insertion, Removal, indicating that the broader group of antepartum interventions is organized into specific actions of applying, inserting, and removing devices or treatments during the prenatal period.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 23 -- Level 3 -- Community 1694
retrieved_docs.content: Procedure on blood vessel is classified as تروية دموية and is a subcategory of other or procedures on vessels of head and neck, indicating that interventions directed at blood vessels are considered vascularization procedures and belong to the broader group of head and neck vessel procedures.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 34 -- Level 2 -- Community 1021
retrieved_docs.content: organizes general obstetric procedure categories (إجراء توليدي, Antepartum Procedures, Obstetrics incision) and specific clinical actions—ranging from diagnostic sampling and membrane management to fetal stimulation, reduction of multiple gestations, and surgical treatment of ectopic pregnancies—providing a comprehensive framework for coordinating obstetric care across the ante‑partum, intrapartum, and postpartum periods.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2431
retrieved_docs.content: Cervical shortening in pregnancy is categorized as a component of other complications of birth and puerperium that influence the management of the mother, and it specifically encompasses the sub‑condition of cervical shortening occurring in the second trimester.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2393
retrieved_docs.content: External obstetrical version is a fetal procedure that is classified among other delivery procedures, indicating that it is a type of intervention performed on the fetus to reposition it prior to birth and is grouped within the broader category of delivery methods.
